ucs.title: [LLM] FIN7 (Sangria Tempest / Carbon Spider) POWERTRASH loader: obfuscated PowerShell spawned from mshta/wscript/Office
ucs.description: Hunts FIN7's signature in-memory PowerShell loader chain (POWERTRASH / POWERPLANT / GRIFFON) where mshta.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, or an Office host launches heavily obfuscated PowerShell with base64/IEX cradles. This is the documented post-spearphish (T1566.001/002) execution pattern for Carbanak-lineage intrusions and is consistently reused across FIN7's BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware operations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","hh.exe") AND Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval cmd_len=len(cmdline) | eval susp_flag=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)(-nop|-noprofile|-w(in)?\s+hidden|-windowstyle\s+hidden|-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String|IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|DownloadData|\[char\]|-join|-split|`[a-z]|\$\{)"),1,0) | where susp_flag=1 OR cmd_len>800 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime host user parent_process_name parent_cmd cmdline cmd_len pid
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "powershell.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","hh.exe")
| extend cmdLen = strlen(ProcessCommandLine)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-nop","-noprofile","-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-enc","-encodedcommand","FromBase64String","IEX","Invoke-Expression","DownloadString","DownloadData")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(\$\{[^}]+\}|`[a-z]|\[char\]|-join|-split|-replace\s)"
   or cmdLen > 800
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, cmdLen, SHA256, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: FIN7's loader family (POWERTRASH, POWERPLANT, GRIFFON, BIRDWATCH, BOOSTWRITE) is delivered via .lnk/.iso/.js droppers that consistently invoke mshta or Windows Script Host to chain into obfuscated PowerShell — Mandiant, Microsoft (Sangria Tempest), and CrowdStrike (Carbon Spider) all flag this exact parent→child relationship as a Carbon Spider hallmark. Generic Office→PowerShell rules miss the msh

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN7 BadUSB / 'badusb' HID drop: explorer-spawned scripting host from removable media
ucs.description: Hunts the FBI-flagged FIN7 mail-in USB campaign (Aug 2022, ongoing through ALPHV operations) where a USB device — either a LNK on a thumb drive or a HID-emulating BadUSB — causes explorer.exe to spawn cmd/powershell/rundll32 referencing a non-system drive letter. Used by FIN7 to bypass email gateways entirely against retail, hospitality, defense, and transport targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?i)(?<drv>[d-z]:\\\\[^\"\s]+\.(lnk|bat|cmd|js|vbs|ps1|hta))" | where isnotnull(drv) OR match(cmdline,"(?i)([d-z]:\\\\.+\.(lnk|bat|cmd|js|vbs|ps1|hta)|powershell.+(IEX|DownloadString|-enc))") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime host user process_name parent_process_name cmdline drv
ucs.kql:
let removableDriveProcs =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)[d-z]:\\[^\"\s]+\.(lnk|bat|cmd|js|vbs|ps1|hta)"
       or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("IEX","DownloadString","-enc","FromBase64String","Invoke-WebRequest")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, ReportId;
let removableLnkDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".lnk" or FileName endswith ".bat" or FileName endswith ".ps1"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Z]:\\"
    | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, LnkPath=FolderPath, LnkFile=FileName;
removableDriveProcs
| join kind=leftouter removableLnkDrops on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or (Timestamp between ((DropTime - 1h) .. (DropTime + 24h)))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, LnkPath, LnkFile
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: The FBI's Aug-2022 FLASH alert and Mandiant's tracking of FIN7 (UNC3753) specifically call out mailed BadUSB / LilyGO devices to US defense, transport, and insurance targets that bridge directly into Diceloader/POWERTRASH and BlackCat ransomware. Generic 'process from USB' detections drown in legitimate IT activity; pinning on explorer.exe parent + script interpreter child + removable-drive path +
